questions.question: Vishnu Vaman Shirwadkar, who was seen in the news, was associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Marathi Bhasha Diwas or Marathi Day is celebrated on February 27 every year on the birth anniversary of eminent Marathi poet Vishnu Vaman Shirwadkar. He was also called as ‘Kusumagraj’. His works including the Vishakha (1942) inspired people into the Indian freedom movement. He was the recipient of several State awards, and National awards including the Sahitya Akademi Award for Natsamrat, Padma Bhushan (1991) and Jnanpith Award.

questions.question: Bhagat Singh Koshyari, who was seen in the news, is the Governor of which state?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra Governor Bhagat Singh Koshyari commented that Samarth Ramdas was the guru of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj. Several historians and Maratha Organisations claim that Shivaji’s mother, Rajmata Jijau, was his real guru and inspired him to create his own kingdom. Hence the followers of Shivaji demanded apology from the Governor. Samarth Ramdas was an Indian Marathi Hindu saint, philosopher and poet.

questions.question: The Barents Sea, which was seen in the news recently, is located along the coasts of which two countries?
questions.explanation: The Barents Sea is a marginal sea of the Arctic Ocean, located along the northern coasts of Norway and Russia. It is also called as the Murman Sea in Russia. Russian nuclear submarines sailed off for drills in the Barents Sea and mobile missile launchers were seen among snow forests in Siberia after President Vladimir Putin ordered his country’s nuclear forces put on high alert over tensions with the West amidst the invasion of Ukraine.

questions.question: A new species of Orchids ‘Maxillaria anacatalinaportillae’ have been discovered from which South American country?
questions.explanation: Scientists have described a new species of the Neotropical orchid genus Maxillaria from northern Ecuador. Named Maxillaria anacatalinaportillae, it grows as an epiphyte in cloud rainforests at altitude of 1,700 m above sea level. According to the IUCN Red List criteria, the species should be classified as ‘Critically Endangered’, as per the Scientists.

questions.question: The ‘Roorkee Water Conclave’ has been organised by IIT Roorkee along with which institution?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Jal Shakti Shri Gajendra Singh Shekhawat inaugurated the 2nd edition of the ‘Roorkee Water Conclave’ today at IIT, Roorkee. It is being jointly organised by Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee and National Institute of Hydrology, focusing on the theme, ‘Water Security for Sustainable Development’. National Institute of Hydrology is an autonomous society under Ministry of Jal Shakti.

questions.question: The ‘Sagar Praikrama’ Program was inaugurated at which state?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Parshottam Rupala inaugurated the ‘Sagar Parikrama’ in Gujarat. It is organised along with National Fisheries Development Board, Department of Fisheries, Gujarat, Indian Coast Guard, Fishery Survey of India, Gujarat Maritime Board and fishermen representatives. It aims to understand the problems of Coastal Fisher folk.

questions.question: Which is the headquarters of United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)?
questions.explanation: The United Nations Environment Programme coordinates responses to environmental issues within the UN system. It was formed after the UN Conference on the Human Environment in Stockholm in June 1972. UNEP is headquartered at Nairobi, Kenya. The UNEP is set to commemorate the 50th anniversary of its creation in a special two-day session of the UN Environment Assembly.

questions.question: What is the objective of the ‘Jan Aushadhi Bal Mitra programme’?

options.label: C
options.text: Provide subsidised medicines to pregnant women

questions.explanation: On the occasion of Jan Aushadhi Diwas week celebrations, Jan Aushadhi Bal Mitra programme has been conducted across the country at 75 locations. It aims to engage children as ‘Bal Mitras’ with Jan Aushadhi scheme to educate them about the benefits of the PM Bharatiya Jan Aushadi Pariyojana.

questions.question: Which country launched ‘Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter’ (LRO)?
questions.explanation: The Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO) is a NASA robotic spacecraft orbiting the Moon in an eccentric polar mapping orbit. NASA’s Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter is set to capture the surface of the Moon, which is to be smacked by a rocket booster at around 6,000 mph. The up-close view of the smoldering crater is expected to shed some light on the mysterious physics of planetary impacts.

questions.question: Indian grey hornbills were recently reintroduced in which Indian sanctuary?
questions.explanation: Forest authorities have released 20 Indian grey hornbills into Gir National Park in Gujarat since October to reintroduce the species. The species was seen at the sanctuary several decades back. Two male birds among those were released with solar-powered satellite transmitters. As many as 10 out of the total 62 species are found in Indian subcontinent.
